channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Super Yooka-Laylee Kart Leaked by Steam DB Listing
item.content:encoded:
As everyone readies for their grand premieres little oopsies continue to happen with leaks spilling out into the public of announcements before they are meant to happen like on SteamDB today which revealed the existence of Super Yooka-Laylee Kart.

After some cheeky teasing last week of Yooka and Laylee piloting something on social media, the SteamDB listing for an 'unknown App' previously known as Super Yooka-Laylee Kart Playtest with images showing the titular reptile and bat racing in high octane action with the title Super Yooka-Laylee Kart. There is a distinct lack of details of what precisely the game will be but using our knowledge of Yooka-Laylee, gaming, and the images we can draw some conclusions.

The first is that this will be, gasp a kart racing game, following the footsteps of many mascot platformers like Super Mario, Sonic The Hedgehog, Donkey Kong, and Crash Bandicoot among many others to shift gears from running and jumping to speeding around tracks in games like Mario Kart, Sonic and All Stars Racing Transformed, Diddy Kong Racing, and Crash Team Racing. We see a colorful cast of characters in the art, as well as a variety of vehicles, with a villainous character looming in the background. 

Characters in the library art capsule include the obvious inclusions of Yooka and Laylee, with the red snake Trowzer behind on the left, and opposite them is Vendi, who is essentially a sentient vending machine. In the air behind Vendi is the half-octopus//half-human Dr Puss and the duck opposite her is Dr Quack, mad scientist, a secondary villain. Finally in center is Capital B, clearly here to stop some fun. 

What sort of approach they are going to take to this kart racer is yet to be seen, and if it will fall in reception more along the lines of the Yooka-Laylee release or its sequel Yooka-Laylee and the Impossible Lair. Most likely seems something safer like your classic Mario Kart racing levels which arguably reached their zenith with Mario Kart 8 and Sonic All Star Racing Transformed. They could opt for a more exploratory approach like the somewhat devisivie Mario Kart Wordl, or they could swing for the fences with a more campaign focused adventure and interactible world and make the spiritual successor to Diddy Kong Racing that people have been waiting almost 30 years for. Or maybe it will be the car constructing game spiritual success for Banjo-Kazooi: Nuts and Bolts that has been so hotly desired.

Expect to hear more sometime this week at one of the upcoming showcases events, and stay tuned to TechRaptor for coverage of all this summer's hottest reveals.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
